prose:
**TEMPO frontier-orbital analysis (HOMO–LUMO gap)** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, PySCF), **gas phase** (no solvent model applied), **charge 0, multiplicity 2** (open-shell doublet radical → UKS). Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz` at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz` (29 atoms), used as-is — this was a single point on the provided geometry, not re-optimized at this level. Density fitting (RI) is on by default in chekit's PySCF backend.

**Headline result:** HOMO–LUMO gap = **3.8724203 eV** (Koopmans-style orbital gap, not an excitation energy).

**Frontier orbital energies:**
- HOMO (index 86, occ 1.0) = −6.3375439 eV
- LUMO (index 87, occ 0.0) = −2.4651235 eV
- HOMO−1 = −6.9286592 eV; HOMO−2 = −7.3057732 eV
- LUMO+1 = +0.58631725 eV; LUMO+2 = +0.59688153 eV

Note the singly-occupied orbitals (occupation 1.0) reflect the open-shell doublet ground state of the TEMPO radical.

**Koopmans-derived descriptors:** vertical IP = 6.3375439 eV, vertical EA = 2.4651235 eV, electronegativity = 4.4013337 eV, chemical hardness = 1.9362102 eV, chemical softness = 0.51647286 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity index = 5.0024886 eV.

**Total electronic energy:** −483.88366 Hartree (−13167.145 eV; −303641.58 kcal/mol), zero = electronic energy (bare nuclei + electrons). Absolute energies of this kind are only comparable to same-method, same-basis values.

**Convergence & integrity:** SCF converged in 12 cycles. Engine integrity status: **ok / trustworthy: true** (checks: energy_finite ok, scf_converged ok, schema_header ok, schema_headline ok). **No warnings were reported by the engine** (the `warnings` array was empty — a clean run).

**Reproducibility:** command run was `.../cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 2 <tempo.xyz> --out .../live.json`. The engine also streams a live `<subcommand>_<timestamp>.out` log to its run directory during execution (`tail -f` for real-time progress); the result JSON was written to `.../.fidelity_scratch/tmp0w1_fvba/live.json`.

No experimental/literature comparison is offered — provenance here is **computed**, not measured. I can verify a reference value against a source if you want one.
